21.correct: The sensation of sound persists in our brain (ears) for about 0.1 second; this is why an echo is heard distinctly only if the reflected sound returns after at least 0.1 s.

wrong.A: 0.01 s is too short for the persistence of hearing.
wrong.C: 1 s is far longer than the actual persistence.

21.fact: This persistence of hearing determines the minimum distance needed for a distinct echo.

22.correct: The Eastern Ghats are also known by local names such as the Nallamalai Hills (in Andhra Pradesh).

wrong.A: The Nilgiri Hills are part of the Western Ghats.
wrong.B: The Rajmahal Hills are in Jharkhand.
wrong.C: The Maikal Hills are part of the Satpura system.

22.fact: The Eastern Ghats are discontinuous, cut across by rivers like the Godavari and Krishna.

23.correct: Trade, investment and finance was among Brazil's key priorities for its BRICS 2025 chairship.

wrong.B: Sports, tourism and youth affairs was not a stated key priority area.
wrong.C: Space exploration cooperation was not the headline priority.
wrong.D: Cultural heritage and language promotion was not the main priority.

23.fact: Brazil chaired BRICS in 2025, hosting its summit and ministerial meetings.

24.correct: High rural literacy reducing the labour supply was NOT a challenge facing Indian agriculture between 1950 and 1990 - in fact rural literacy was low and labour was abundant.

wrong.A: Fragmented landholdings were a genuine challenge.
wrong.B: Dependence on monsoon rainfall was a real challenge.
wrong.C: Low use of fertilizers and irrigation was a genuine problem.

24.fact: The Green Revolution addressed some of these challenges through HYV seeds, fertilizers and irrigation.

25.correct: The Third Five-Year Plan was terminated a year early because of the disruption caused by the 1962 India-China war (and later wars/droughts).

wrong.D: The Fourth Plan came after a 'plan holiday'.

25.fact: After the Third Plan, India had a three-year 'plan holiday' (1966-69).

26.correct: The Chalukyas of Badami are known for the rock-cut cave temples at Badami, an important contribution to Indian temple architecture.

wrong.A: The bronze Nataraja is associated with the Cholas.
wrong.C: The Nagara temple style developed mainly in the north.
wrong.D: The Ellora paintings are linked to other dynasties (Rashtrakutas).

26.fact: Badami, Aihole and Pattadakal form the cradle of Chalukyan temple architecture.

27.correct: National income measured at constant (base-year) prices is called real income (real GDP/national income), as it removes the effect of price changes.

wrong.B: Nominal income is measured at current prices.
wrong.C: Disposable income is income after taxes.
wrong.D: Net factor income is a separate concept.

27.fact: Real income reflects the actual change in the volume of goods and services produced.

28.correct: The Charter Act of 1853 first allowed Indians to be recruited to the Indian Civil Service through open competition held in London.

wrong.A: The 1813 Charter Act dealt with the Company's monopoly and education funds.
wrong.B: The 1833 Charter Act centralised administration under the Governor-General.
wrong.D: The 1861 Act dealt with legislative councils.

28.fact: The first Indian to qualify for the ICS was Satyendranath Tagore in 1863.

29.correct: The 'Navya' programme (June 2025) aims to provide vocational training to adolescent girls aged 16-18 years.

wrong.A: It is not specifically about higher education for girls.
wrong.C: It is not a sports-promotion programme.
wrong.D: It is not a financial-assistance scheme.

29.fact: Navya is a joint initiative to build employable skills among adolescent girls.

30.correct: As of August 2025, about 84,902 sewer and septic-tank workers (SSWs) had been profiled/validated under the NAMASTE scheme.
30.fact: NAMASTE aims to ensure the safety and dignity of sanitation workers and end hazardous manual cleaning.

31.correct: The Bhortal Nritya (Bortal dance), performed with large cymbals, belongs to Assam.

wrong.A: Gendi is a tribal dance of central India.
wrong.B: Kayang is associated with other regions.
wrong.C: This option does not name an Assamese dance.

31.fact: The Bhortal dance was popularised by the dancer Narahari Burha Bhakat in Assam.

32.correct: India's first census of coastal and wader birds began in January 2025 at Jamnagar (Gujarat).

wrong.A: It did not begin at Raipur.
wrong.B: It did not begin at Jammu.
wrong.C: It did not begin at Chennai.

32.fact: Jamnagar's Marine National Park and surrounding wetlands host many migratory and wader birds.

33.correct: The 'Godachi Fair' (Godachi Veerabhadreshwara Jatra) is traditionally celebrated in Karnataka.

wrong.A: It is not a Gujarat fair.

33.fact: It is one of the largest fairs in northern Karnataka, dedicated to Lord Veerabhadra.

34.correct: As per the 2011 Census, Kerala's literacy rate was about 94%, the highest among Indian states.
34.fact: Kerala consistently ranks highest in literacy and human-development indicators in India.

35.correct: Rice is cultivated in both the seasons referred to (including the kharif season) across different Indian states depending on climate and irrigation - so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.B: 'Only II' (only kharif) ignores rice grown in other seasons in some states.

35.fact: Rice is mainly a kharif crop but is grown in the rabi/summer season too in states like West Bengal and the south.

36.correct: The first season of the Indian Premier League (IPL) was played in 2008.

wrong.B: 2009 was the second season (held in South Africa).

36.fact: Rajasthan Royals won the inaugural 2008 IPL title.

37.correct: The Volleyball Federation of India was formed in 1951.
37.fact: Volleyball is a popular sport in many Indian states, played at school and national levels.

38.correct: The skill called 'toe touch' is associated with the sport of kabaddi, where a raider quickly touches a defender's toe and retreats.

wrong.A: It is not a basketball term.
wrong.C: It is not a football term.
wrong.D: It is not a badminton term.

38.fact: The toe-touch is a common raiding technique to score a point in kabaddi.

39.correct: The Global Business Complexity Index 2025 was published by the TMF Group.

wrong.B: The IMF did not publish it.
wrong.C: The WTO did not publish it.
wrong.D: The Economist Intelligence Unit did not publish this index.

39.fact: The index ranks jurisdictions by how complex it is to do business there.

40.correct: The Government notified the National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) for 12 pollutants in 2009.

wrong.D: 2015 was when the AQI was launched, not the NAAQS notification.

40.fact: The NAAQS are enforced by the Central Pollution Control Board.
